videos.title: US-Iran Tensions Flare Ahead of Trump-Xi Meeting | Balance of Power 05/11/2026
videos.description:
"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Representative Jill Tokuda (D-HI), warns Taiwan "must not be a bargaining chip" during President Donald Trump's talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Tom Kloza, Chief Energy Advisor at Gulf Oil, says reopening the Strait of Hormuz is "the only lever you can pull that has a major impact" on fuel prices, warning gasoline could reach $5 a gallon or higher this summer if it stays closed. Mara Rudman, Professor at the University of Virginia's Miller Center and former Deputy Envoy for Middle East Peace at the US State Department, says there is likely no "near-term" diplomatic solution with Iran.

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Balance of Power: Late Edition Begins
00:01:58 - Jeff Mason On Pres. Trump Saying US-Iran Ceasefire on ‘Massive Life Support,’ Executives Going to China With White House
00:03:46 - Rep. Jill Tokuda (D) Hawaii on US-Iran Tensions Flare Ahead of Trump-Xi Meeting
00:13:22 - Power Moves: Top Financial Stories of the Day
00:17:07 - Tom Kloza, Gulf Oil Chief Oil Advisor, Shares His Insight on Pres. Trump Floats Temporary Gas Tax Halt
00:25:25 - Enda Curran on Jerome Powell Wraps Up Fed Chair Tenure, Traders Brace for Kevin Warsh
00:28:21 - Mara Rudman, Prof. at the Univ. of Virginia Miller Center, on Reaction From Trump’s Rhetoric of Iran Negotiations, Ceasefire on ‘Massive Life Support’
00:35:14 - Power Brief: Top Stories Watching Tonight
00:38:11 - Political Panel React to Poll Saying 24% People Think WHCD Shooting Was Staged, Is Trump Ballroom on Legislation Table, Federal Gas Tax Holiday
00:45:30 - Breaking News: CNN Says Supreme Court Lets Alabama Change House Map
--------

videos.description:

videos.title: Oil Gains as Trump Signals Iran Deal On 'Life Support' | Bloomberg Businessweek Daily 5/11/2026
videos.description:
Bloomberg's Devika Krishna Kumar breaks down the latest movement in the oil markets as Trump throws cold water on hopes of a deal between the US and Iran. Then, Hardika Singh, economic strategist at Fundstrat, discusses the markets as stocks continue to trade higher despite risk from the war in Iran. Plus, Bloomberg Economics chief geoeconomics analyst Jennifer Welch previews Trump's trip to Beijing to meet with China's President Xi later this week. Finally, Angi CEO Jeff Kip talks his company's 'radical pivot' to AI.

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Businessweek Daily
00:03:54 - Oil Climbs After Trump Rebuffs Iran's Offer
00:06:13 - Trump Floats Temporary Gas Tax as Prices Soar
00:10:22 - Oil Climbs as Trump Says US-Iran Ceasefire on 'Life Support'
00:12:48 - Global Economy Feels Impact of Iran War
00:21:16 - Trump Rejects New Iran Offer
00:23:01 - Trump to meet Xi in Beijing this Week
00:28:47 - Musk, Cook Set to Join Trump for Xi Summit, White House Says
00:34:12 - Angi Becomes Latest Company to Announce AI Pivot 
00:43:42 - Stocks on the Move Today

videos.title: Keir Starmer Delivers Speech to Save His Premiership (Full Remarks)
videos.description:
Prime Minister Keir Starmer has delivered a last-ditch effort to save his premiership this morning, with a politically charged address designed to hold back calls for a change in leadership atop Britain’s governing Labour Party. During the speech, he reiterated his message from Friday that he won’t walk away and risk “plunging the country into chaos.” The prime minister also addressed questions about his leadership. “I know people are frustrated with me,” he said. “I know I have doubters, and I know I need to prove them wrong.” Starmer also vowed to put Britain “at the heart of Europe” and announced the nationalisation of British Steel.
--------
